title: Memorandum of Disbursements
summary: Notations of various payments

title: Loan of Money
translation_text: Aurelius Pemes, son of Patas and his mother Tanouphis, from the village of Karanis, about 45 years of age, with a scar on his right shin, to Aurelius Sarapammon, (formerly?) gymnasiarch, councillor, former strategus of the city of the Arsinoites, greetings. I acknowledge through the present document that I have received in loan from you in cash ten talents and three thousand drachmas in silver, in total 10 Tal., 3000 dr., for one month from the present day on, which I shall of necessity return on the 15th of the coming month Thoth, without interest forthwith. And if I fail to return them on the due date, I shall be subject to the (payment of) interest for the surpassing period and on demand you, the said Sarapammon, shall have the right of execution against both me, the acknowledging party Pemes, and against all my property, as if in consequence of a court decision. The document, written in a single copy, must be authoritative and in answer to the formal question I replied positively. In the consulship of our lord Constantinus Augustus (consul) for the 7th time and our lord Constantius, the most noble Caesar (consul) for the 1st time, Mesore 20. (M. 2) I Aurelius Pemes, have received the said ten talents and three thousand drachmas in silver and I shall return them on the due date in full as stated above. I, Aurelius Serenos, son of Heron, wrote for him, since he does not know letters. (Verso) Note of hand of Pemes from Karanis ...

title: Letter from a Churchman
translation_text: After leaving the town I shall go first to Takona and I shall travel up to the monastery of the Holy Georgios and there I shall take on a cargo. I looked for Mousaios, the child of Staurophania, because of the adaeratio I had provided (?) for him, and I found out to which place he had fled and look, I also forwarded to you his letter concerning the things he had sent me after he fled, in order that you too read it and (the letter) of our lord the scholasticus Bacchus. For you know well that I have his pledge that he will answer him. And look, I have written one letter to the same lord Bacchus in order that he comes to Takona and inspects his possessions. Please, ge hold not of the letter of Mousais and send it to me in order that get hold of it as testimony. (Verso) To my God-guarded lord and brother N.N., son of Phoibammon, ---, chartularius, Georgios, deacon, ---

title: Hyponema for the Nomarch Nikanor
summary: Four separate documents
date_text: III BCE: June 30, 237 or June 24, 212 or June 20, 195 BCE?
translation_text: (Verso) Memorandum to Nikanor, nomarch, from Heryothes. (M. 3) If it seems right to you, please, give an order to Achoapis concerning Petosiris, son of Thotortaios, a farmer from Philadelphia, in order that he be released (from jail) after a surety has been provided and that he will be there for the collecting of the wheat and --- on the threshing floors. He cultivates 55 arouras of land. (M. 4) Nikanor to Achoapis, greeting. After having checked whether he is not subject to a rather serious accusation and after having given a surety that he will stay, release him from jail in order that he will be present at the collecting of wheat. Farewell. (Recto) --- to Achoapis ---. (M. 5) To Alexandros. Achaopis to Alexander, greeting. I have sent you the letter from Nicanor. Act now according ---. Year 10, Pachon 13. ---.

title: Account
translation_text: (1-17) [...] of plows: 2 knidia of wine; [...] to the same persons for the treatment of another skin for a yoke band (for) wagons or...: 2 knidia of wine; to the same people for the treament of [N] small skins given to the cowherds, i.e. to Apollon son of Tesneus, 1, to Parous junior cowherd, 1, to Herminos cowherd, 1, together: 3 knidia of wine; to the same shoemakers for the treatment of other small skins for the use of donkey drivers carrying sacks...... skins: 4 knidia of wine; [...] through Paesis and N.N., camel drivers, during the transport of wine of the (village of) Ares: 2 knidia of wine; together 20 knidia of wine; total of 10 ... On account of chaff for the value of chaff, of wine as declared in the wine expense [...] loads; and in money, likewise, as declared in the money expense [...] (trans. ed. pr.)

title: Official Document from the time of Septimius Severus
Contract of loan with mortgage
translation_text: --- (at an interest of one drachma) per each mna for each month from the current month of --- (and) of necessity I shall pay back (the principal and the monthly interest) until the 10th of the month of Thoth of the coming nth year (without any delay); but if I fail to do so, I acknowledge that there remain with you, instead of the principal and the interest, the possession and ownership for ever of the nth part belonging to me of a house, situated in the Thoeris Street quarter, and an atrium and courtyard and the entrances and the exits and the appurtenances and all the annexes, the boundaries of which are: to the South: ---, to the North: property of Alia alias Diogenis, to the East: ---, to the West: (property) of (N.N., son/daughter of) Ptollarion and however (s)he is styled --- the aforementioned house and all the appurtenances --- and the power to use it and to dispose of it in whatever way you may choose, and neither I nor anyone else on my behalf shall have the right to make a claim upon it or any part of it in any way whatsoever, and I shall further deliver it to you guaranteed perpetually against all claims with every guarantee and free from persons' property returns and (the liability to) cultivate royal or estate domain land from every obligation or debt or lien of every kind public or private and from everything else whatsoever; I will of necessity and at once repel ever person who in any way whatsoever proceeds against or claims this property, whether whole or part, at my own costs, as if in consequence of a legal decision ---.

title: Account
translation_text: Account of salary and wages we have received; 1st installment 800 drachmae, 2nd installment 340 drachmae; 3rd installment 260 drachmae; 4th installment 200 drachmae.

title: Fragmentary letter from a ship captain (?)
translation_text: -- I have handed over 400 empty new jars in number, 100 (jars) capable of being heated (?). I made the ship depart today at the 3rd hour because of the animals. Akylas has given only the matter (money/fodder?) of the animals. The have done the remaining --. Hand over to Aurelius Eoni-- son of Alexander (?) / Alexandrian (?) from Theon.

title: Tax receipt
genre: Papyri; Documentary; Poll Tax; Pig Tax; Receipt
translation_text: Year 15 of Hadrian Caesar the lord, Pachon 7. (In the quarter) of the Anamphodarchi, Diokles, slave of Pausirion son of Theon, has paid through Xenon collector of the laographia, for the laographia of the 15th year, 4 dr. total 4 dr.; for the pig tax 1 dr., 5 obols 1/2 obol, total 1 dr., 5 1/2 ob. For the laographia of the 16th year, Phamenoth [ ], (in the quarter) of the Anamphodarchi the same (Diokles has paid) 8 dr., total 8 dr. For the 16th year, Pachon 27, (in the quarter) of the Anamphodarchi the same (Diokles has paid) for the laographia of the 16th year, 4 dr.; for the pig tax 1 dr., 5 obols, 1/2 obol, total: 1 dr., 5 1/2 ob.

title: Report Concerning Work Peformed
translation_text: Of Apollodors ... during the month Thoth days n, during the month Phaophi days n., during the month Hathyr days n and during the month Choiak until the 11th, 9 days; 12th: he worked, 13th: he worked, etc.

title: Order for Delivery
translation_text: Johannes, vir clarissimus, to Phoibammon, butcher. Deliver to Papnouthios and Psaeios, sailors, going to Tychinphagon on account of the embole of the 5th indiction (?) n measure of chaff (?). Phamenoth 29, indiction n. I have signed.

title: Contract for a Parachoresis
genre: Papyri; Documentary; Contract; Land cession; Parachoresis
translation_text: Year fourteen of Nero Claudius Caesar August Germanicus Imperator, on Pachon the tenth, in the city of Oxyrhynchoi. N.N. daughter of N.N. son of Kephalas and of Tausarapis daughter of Horus, with as her guardian her husband (?) N.N. son of N.N. from --, acknowledges to Berenice, daughter of N.N. son of Dionysios and of Berenice daughter of Dio--, from ---, that she has ceded to her, in accordance with the administrative arrangements of those controlling cavalry affairs and on the basis of a memorandum submitted by her to the official in charge of the registry of military settlers' land grants, three arouras of catoicic land and land bought for conversion into catoicic land, belonging to her near Nesla in the Upper Toparchy in the kleros of N.N., together with the yearly rents thereof, of which the neighbors are: to the South --, to the North --, to the West --, to the East --, in order that the three arouras shall belong to Berenice and her offspring along with the other rights and benefits, irrefutably for all time, in accordance with the ordinances and instructions, and the she, the acknowledging party, has received from Berenice the agreed upon among of cession money, i.e. n silver drachmas of imperial money, on the spot and in full, at the moments when this was urgent, and that regarding this cession N.N. will not violate the contract and will not attempt any fraud, in any way whatsoever, and that the same (woman) will absolutely hand over to Berenice or to her representatives the three arouras fully guaranteed and free from all the public imposts and other dues, from previous years up to the 5th of the Epogomenai of Mesore of the past thirteenth year and including the thirteenth year of Nero Claudius Caesar August Germanicus Imperator. If she violates any of these provisions in the contract her action will be invalid and she shall forfeit to Berenice or to her representatives for every specific breach both the damages and a fine of n silver drachmas and to the state the sum, and nevetheless the contract as drawn up in accordance with their mutual harmonious agreement, shall be binding. (M. 2) In the 14th year of Nero Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus Imperator, on Pachon the tenth. It has been registered through N.N., tax-farmer (representing his colleagues). (Translation of P.NYU II 15a)

title: Private Account
translation_text: Year 17. List of net (?) revenues (or: 'list of Philon, of revenues'): dr. 76 (ex 96); of which were expended for the price of wine on the 4th for 3 4-chous jars 27 drachmas; likewise, on the 5th for 2 4-chous jars 18 dr., for the conveyance 1 ob., for top( ) 4 ob., makes 18 dr., 5 ob. On the 6th, for 4 4-chous jars 36 dr., for their conveyance 2 ob., for top( ) 6 ob., makes 37 drachmas, 1 ob., of which for --- 18 dr., for other expenditures 9 dr., for other expenditures for/to Kastor 4 dr., makes 31 dr., leaves (?) 6 dr., 1 ob.

translation_text: concerning Zoilos and ---- having obtained --- the writing fee (?) of the office of the agoranomos we shall give as written above. The personal signature of each of us is authoritative. In year one of the Imperator Nerva Caesar Traianus Augustus, Pauni 5. I, Asklepiades son of Klaros, have (received?) together with the others two talents and on receipt of the balance we will cancel the loan on all aforementioned conditions. I, Ptolemaios son of Sarapion have (received?) together with others two talents and on receipt of the balance we will cancel the loan on all afore-mentioned conditions. I, Herodes son of Sarapion, have (received?) together with the others two talents and receipt of the balance we will cancel the loan on all afore-mentioned conditions. Year one of Imperator Nerva Caesar Traianus Augustus, Pauni 5. (M. 2) --tos son of Zoilos have produced -- and I have the handwritten original ---. Year six of Imperator Caesar Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus Epeiph 5.

Diogenes, vineyard land --- 15+ ar.; Agathinus son of Polemon, for vineyard land --- ar.; for (the village of) Sinkepha for (land owned by) inhabitants of the provincial metropolis ---; Horion alias Berenicianus through his manager ---; N.N. child of Diogenes, for private land ---; the heirs of Apion son of Euangelos, --- ar.; --oros son of Dios, 1 2/3 of private uninundated land --; ---

translation_text: --Agathos Daimon, for 10 1/4 1/32 arouras of vineyard land, 512 dr. 0.5 ob.; For 1 aroura of garden land, 28 dr. 2 ob.; To -- and/alias Demetrios and -- of --iaina, for 15 [1/-] 1/16 1/32 ar. of vineyard land, 853 dr., 3 ob. 2 chalki; -- for 1/16 (?) aroura of garden land; -- for the time agreed upon; -- of --aip( ), for 7 arouras of vineyard land, 396 dr. 4 ob. -- e( ) child of Thatres, for 7 1/4 1/8 1/64 ar. of vineyard land, 447 dr., 1 ob.-- .

title: Settlement of a dispute (dialysis)
translation_text: Aurelius Herakles, officially described as son of his mother Tapontos, to Aurelius Chairemon, son of Menodoros. Since it seemed good (?) to us who have been mentioned in the meantime to settle the differences between us, i.e., between you and me, who have leased a vineyard in the neighborhood of Ision Panga, we came, together with the common mediators, to a settlement as has been written below. It seemed good concerning the irrigatoin of the n arourae to the West of the vineyard that I would receive for the irrigation per aroura thirty-two silver drachmas, regarding the chaff --- we settled on 30 mouia -- measured the -- to be stored away -- to my satisfaction -- me Heraklios -- Harmiysis -

title: Declaration of sheep and goats
translation_text: (M. 2) At (?) Sepho. (M. 1) To Hierax, strategus, from Amatokos son of Dikaiogenes. I register for the present 7th year of Tiberius Caesar Augustus the twenty eight sheep, two goats, total 28 sheep, 2 goats, which belong to us, and the accompanying lambs and kids which will graze in the neighborhood of Sepho in the Thmoisepho toparchy and through the whole nome, the shepherd being N.N., son of Deios, registered at (the village of) Nesmimis, and for which I shall also pay the proper tax. --- [ed. pr.]

title: Fragments of a codex of accounts
Official Document
genre: Papyri; Documentary; Petition; Application; Magister rei publicae
translation_text: --- from Aurelius/Flavius/Valerius Antonius Theodoros the most distinguished catholicus (?) and magister rei privatae. As the sale of estates belonging to the fisces and of land parcels --- falling to the most sacred fiscus is, according to custom, coming to be held here in the Capitolium surely on the 11th day before the calends of September (=22. viii), it was necessary to make it clear to all through this proclamation that all those who are willing to make a purchase from the most sacred fiscus should address themselves at my office and ... on the same day, in order that they do (not?) make...clear.

title: Accounts
summary: Administrative document concerned with land
provenance: Found at: Karanis; probably from Oxyrynchite nome

title: Copy of a Sale of a Female Donkey
genre: Papyri; Documentary; Contract; Sale; Female Donkey
translation_text: Phallas, son of Hatres and mother N.N., from the city of Oxyrhynchus, acknowledge that he has sold to Herakleides, son of Didymus, grandson of/alias (?) N.N., his mother is Helena, from Aphrodison in the Small Oasis a female, white, full-grown donkey without a brand, for an agreed price of three hundred twenty drachmas of minuted imperial silver coinage which acknowledging party, Pallas, son of Hatres, has received on the spot in full from the said Herakleides and (Pallas acknowledges) that he will guarantee (the donkey). And if somebody lays his hands on it saying he is the owner, the acknowledging party will remove him at his own expenses or - if he does not - he will hand back to the said Herakleides the afore-mentioned price which has been paid to him. The (contract of) sale is authoritative. Year 7 of Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Caesares the lords Augusti on the 2nd of the month Hadrianus. I, Pallas, son of Hatres, have sold the said donkey and I have received the said three hundred twenty drachmas of silver as stated above. I, Paesis, son of Ploutarchos, from the city of Oxyrhynchus, have written for him as he does not know letters.

title: Tax receipt with seal
translation_text: I received through Menas, soldier, for demosia and diagraphe of the tenth indiction 1/6 'reckoned' solidus, a sixth, in all. In the month Pharmouthi, (day) 1, of the 10th indiction. Claudius agrees. I, Victor apakt( ), wrote at his request for him.

title: Account of tax collections
summary:
The papyrus appears to be complete, but the first column is mutilated and the writing on the verso is much abraded. The hand of the recto is the common slanting cursive of the period; that of the verso is more upright and skilled. The ink is considerably faded on both sides. The text of the recto is a list, in three columns, of tax payments for a tenth indiction. A column of writing on the verso, consisting of 17 lines, may be a continuation of the recto account, but the illegibility
                                                   of the caption makes it impossible to determine this with certainty. Like the recto, it records payments in talents and drachmas, and most of the same taxpayers reappear; despite the general mutilation the following names can be read with fair assurance in lines 4 -6 and 9-17: Abaous son of Paesios, Serapion s. of Harpalos, Papeis s. of Aion, Chaireas s. of Vallas, Kopres s. of Heras, Serapion s. of Patas, Ptollas s. of Venaphris, Chairemon, Aphous [sic] s. of Tiberinus, Seuthes s. of Ptelemaios [sic], Ptollas, Sarapion s. of Casianus. To the right of this verso column, apparently in still another (smaller) hand, are the beginnings of three lines; these look like the remains of a brief notation, perhaps a docket to or memorandum about the whole document. F arther to the right there were once one or two more colums of writing, from which nothing is now recoverable.The recto account, which is reproduced below, records taxpayers' payments, most of them made in
                                                   money (talents and drachmas) and a few in pounds of meat. The payments are listed under three captions: "Collection of money taxes of the 10th indiction" (lines 1-2), "And to the chaff account" (line 36 ), "And to the gifts account of the 10th indiction, collected by the sitologos Antiouris" (lines 43-44). What precisely is meant by this last caption is not certain. The terms 6cx)..),(cx, 6cxU6,;, have been
                                                   encountered before in the papyri, but always in the sense of "presents," usually given by a tenant to his landlord, sometimes on the occasion of a religious festival: e.g. PAmh 93 (=W'Chr 314), 11-12 (cf. S. Eitrem, Symbolae Osloenses 17 (1937], pp. 41-45 ; P. Derchain, Chronique d'Egypte 30 (1955], pp. 324-26 suggests that the term may be "decalque en grec" from Egyptian; see also J. Herrmann,
                                                   Studien zur Bodenpacht [ =Munchener Beitriige 41], pp. 115-16). 12 contains the first occurrence of the term in a context suggesting a tax or regular payment. Possibly we are to understand that the money collected under this heading went into a fund earmarked for festival purposes. Professor J. H. Oliver
                                                   calls my attention to the parallel development of sportulae from illicit tips into legitimate fees (cf. A. H. M. Jones, Studies in Roman Government and Law, pp. 170-72 = Journal of Roman Studies 39 (1949)], pp. 50-51 ). Nos. 12, 15, 16, and 17 are tied closely together by the names which they share with one another. Some of the individua ls named in these lists are found also in PCair Isid, which suggests that the tenth indiction of 12 an<l 16 is that of 321 /2 A.D.(from ed. pr.)

title: Account of grain receipts and shipments
summary:
The papyrus is complete, but the left half is badly worm-eaten. The hand is a characteristic smooth, upright cursive of the period.The text, in two columns, is apparently a record of deliveries to and from the granary, credited to the Ptolemais sector of Karanis for an eighth indiction. Column I lists grain received from individuals on Epeiph 11th. Column II is a list of donkey-loads of grain dispatched from the granary on Epeiph 11th and 14th for delivery to a river port (cf. in trod. to 5-11 a). One donkey carried one sack containing three artabs. On the evidence for identifying the eighth indiction here as that of 319/20 A.D., see the introd. to 12.
                                  The context suggests, further, that the Antiouri(o)s of Column II and 12, 44 is to be identified with the sitologos of Ptolemais who in 318 A.D. issued the receipt in PCair Isid 59.

title: Letter
translation_text: To my lord father Heron, Kopres (sends) greetings. Before all else I pray to (our) god that this letter from me finds you on delivery in good health and in good spirits. As you sent me (word) on the twentieth of Choiak, I have come especially for the shift. So don't think that I am holding back the laborers, but the epimeletes is the one who is holding them back. But do you too exert yourself before all else to send the shift. If you do not send the shift on the appointed day as you arranged with me, as soon as the day of the shift arrives I will come to you. My greetings to all our family by name. Also, if you want to send Alypis for the stones, send him, for I have found them at eighteen talents each stone. (2nd hand) I pray that you fare well for many years. (Address on verso) To my lord father Heron, (from) Kopres.

title: Receipts for tax payments
translation_text: Payni 15th. Aurelius Timotheos, agent of(?) Seleukios the one-eyed, riparius. I have received on behalf of Aion four months’ wages for the twelfth indiction. I, Aurelius Theonas son of Heras, wrote for him, in his presence, because he is illiterate. (3rd hand) Paid by Aion, for clothing of the 7th indiction(?) to Patermouthis, 3000 talents. (2nd hand) Aurelius Antonius aide of Theoninos. I have received on behalf of Aion son of Sarapion, for the twelfth indiction, one month’s wages in full. Peukeis, clerk, I have signed (this receipt for) 6000(?) talents. [ed. pr.]

title: Certificate for performance of irrigation corvee
Receipts for delivery of wheat
summary: The papyrus is complete but badly mutilated at top and bottom. Only the upper third of the recto is written upon. The hand is a rapid slanting cursive. To the left of line 8 are visible the final strokes of three lines of a preceding column, ending σεσ (ημ). (ed. pr.)

title: Account of tax payments
translation_text: Assessment in the month of Hathyr for Choiak. Valerius 23,300 talents, keph( ) 3,000 tal., Heras 9,700 tal., keph( ) 3,000 tal., Aion 14,000 tal., heirs of Tarnalei and Alia 4,000 tal., Tapaeis 8,750 tal., total 66,450 tal. [ed. pr.]

title: Receipt for delivery of wheat
summary:
The papyrus is much damaged. The writing has practically disappeared from the upper half of the
                                  sheet, which apparently contained two receipts for deliveries of foodstuffs. The hand is upright, small and rather cramped. (ed. pr.)

title: Receipt for delivery of grain
summary: Written in an upright, unusually skilled and careful hand, this fragment of a receipt for delivery of grain is one of two texts in this group that bear Christian symbols (cf. also 10, intr.). On the abbreviation χμγ, the meaning of which is still uncertain, see PMerton 94, rn., and A. D. Nock, Speculum 26 (1951), p. 505 note II. Other instances of this Christian symbol in papyri as early as the fourth century are found in several Michigan documents, also from Karanis: PMich 378 (where cf. In.) and 519, and Aegyptus 33 (1953), pp. 17-23. (ed. pr.)

title: Copy of a land declaration
translation_text: (Lines 3-15) I, Aurelius Atisios, registered the above-indicated arouras and I accepted the measurement as aforestated, swearing the imperial oath. I, Aurelius --son of --ios, wrote for him since he is illiterate. I, Aurelius Aphrodisios, surveyor, together with Paulinus co-surveyor, measured the above-mdicated arouras of Atisios. I, Aurelius Paulinus, surveyor, together with Aphrodisios, co-surveyor, measured the above-indicated arouras of Atisios. I, Aurelius Apollonios, bouleutes, iurator, was present at the measurement. I, Aurelius Kopres, bouleutes, iurator, was present at the measurement. I, Aurelius Heron, bouleutes,~iurator, was present at the measurement. I, Aurelius Sarapion, assistant of the dekaprotoi of the toparchy, took cognizance of the measurement. I, Aurelius Pannous, horiodiktes, marked out all the above-indicated arouras and omitted nothing. Aurelius --the elder, didaskalos, this was executed before me.

title: Receipt for delivery of meat
translation_text: Mecheir 29. Valerius son of Antiourios delivered (for the account) of the villagers of Karanis for the first indiction 25 pounds of meat only. I Elias have signed.

title: Loan of wheat
translation_text: Aurelia Tetoueis daughter of Hatres, inhabitant of the village of Karanis, acknowledges to Aurelia Koutina daughter of Elias, inhabitant of the city of Arsinoë, that the acknowledging party has received nine artabs (9 art.) of wheat including interest of one-half, which I will perforce repay without delay on the appointed day in the month of Payni of the current third indiction, in quality fresh from the harvest, free from dirt, unadulterated, and acceptable, and that on formal demand Koutina is to have the right of execution both upon the acknowledging parties and also upon all his property just as if in accordance with a legal judgment, and on formal interrogation I have acknowledged it. In the 4th consulship of our lords Valentinianus and Valens, the eternal Augusti. I, Aurelia Tetoueis, have received the nine artabs—including interest of one-half—of wheat, and I will repay on the appointed day as aforestated. I, Aurelius Hol, have written for him in his presence since he is illiterate. (Docket on verso) Note of hand. Titoueis daughter of Hatres, inhabitant of the village of Karanis, 9 art. wheat.

title: Registration of payments
translation_text: Col. II (M. 2) 24. Didrachmia[ ]. (M. 1) The Aureliai Stratonike alias Sosipatra and Apollonia alias Dieus, both daughters of Saras, ex-gymnasiarch (?) and town councillor of the most glorious city of the Alexandrines and however he is styled, to Aurelius Theon, son of Didymos (ex?-)Exegetes, town councillor and his colleague, public bankers of Oxyrhynchus, greetings. We have paid to you on the current day, for the accounting period of Thoth, for z(eugmatika?), the 2-drachmas tax and the 4-drachmas tax on vineyards, for the revenues of the past 1st year, i.e. for the Eastern toparchy 2 dr., for the Western toparchy 17 dr., for the central toparchy, meris of Alexander (?) 26 dr., makes a total of the payment 45 dr. Year 2 fo the imperatores Caesares Publius Licinius Valerianus and Publius Licinius Valerianus Gallienaus Pii Felices Augusti, Phaophi n. I, Aurelius Saras and however he is styled, have signed through me, Theon.

title: Letter of introduction
translation_text: Proklos sends many greetings to his dearest friend Tryphon. Isidoros the bearer of this letter to you belongs to my household; he originates, however, from the village of Psophthis in the Memphite nome and pays his poll-tax and his other public dues there. Now, after being arrested by your men he was forced to give a declaration under oath with regard to his cultivating five and a half arouras of revenue land near Philadelphia belonging to the estate of Livia. I ask you therefore also on my account, if you care at all for my friendship, leave him undisturbed and take care that the declaration on oath be returned to him. And by doing this you'll gratify me. Farewell. Year 35 of Caesar, Mecheir 25.

title: Account of tax collections in barley and in money
summary: The papyrus is complete in its outer dimensions, but is considerably damaged in the lower half. The writing-in a clear, medium-sized, slightly slanting cursive- is in three columns. The surface of Column I is badly abraded. In Columns II and III, on the other hand, the damage to the papyrus has caused no loss of written text, which is confined to the upper third of the sheet. In Columns I-II are listed payments of barley made by a group of individuals (members of a pittakion?) during Mesore 12-16. Colunm III lists money payments made by the same persons on Mesore I3.Since the only caption is that which at the head of Column I identifies the document as an "Account of Collection of Barley", we are probably to understand that these tax payments were made partly in kind and partly through adaeratio, which is recorded in contemporary documents at the rate of1000 drachmas per artab (cf. PCair Isid 58, introd.). On the date of this account see the intr. to 12.

title: Tax (?) Receipt
translation_text: Year 4 of Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix August, Epeiph 30. Aurelius Horos, son of N.N., chomatepistates of the village of Toou in the Aphroditopolite nome to Aurelius Panetbeuis, son of Hatres, and mother N.N.; I have received from you in full the ---- which were imposed on you.

title: Fragment of a loan
translation_text: (N.N., daughter of N.N., n years old, physical description, has lent to N.N., son of N.N.) (l. 2) n years old, with a scar near the right-hand thumb, one hundred silver drachmas at an interest of 1 drachma per mna, each month, and ten +? artabas of -- barley with the eighth measure of her granary and the borrower must give (the amount borrowed) back to N.N. or (?) to her representatives in the month of --

title: Fragment of a sale contract
translation_text: --. The agreement shall be authoritative wherever it is produced. And any other person who acts as a representative of Horos in this matter shall be (regarded as) competent. Witnesses: Theon --- son of Herakleides, Thracian; Demetrios --- son of Tryphon, belonging to those stationed in Aphrodite; --- Zoilos son of Hekataios, Thracian. --- belonging to those stationed in Touphis ---; Proxenos son of Proxenos, Thracian. D- (son of N.N.)---. Syngraphophylax: Proxenos, son of Proxenos ---. I, Horos, agree to have sold --- and received the price as written and I have deposited the contract in so far as it is authoritative with the syngraphophylax Proxenos. (M. 2) I, Proxenos, have received (the document in so far as it is) authoritative.

title: Letter to Tetseiris
translation_text: --- (l. 2) your mother thanks you. Melistanos, your son, greets you and Murismos and your mother Aphrodisia and Herminus and Kichois and all (the women) in the shop. Greet Muron, do everything in order to come out quickly. I pray for your health, sister. (Verson) Give to Tetseiris from --.

title: Petition
translation_text: To Alkibiades, chief of the guards (?), from Kallistratos son of Kallistratos, the superintendent of the donkeys on the estate of Livia and Germanicus Caesar. In the month Choiak of the past 34th regnal year of Caesar I took into service Pe--- son of ---nis as donkey driver in order to take care of my donkey animals and to stay with them during the harvest period until the month of Choiak of the 35th regnal year of Caesar and to accomplish everything that is fitting for a donkey driver; the person indicated was in agreement. After having stayed around during the winter of the 34th regnal year and during the harvest period he was unlawfully deducting from the freight charges the money intended for the donkey driving business, while disregarding what is right in the first place, as he did not take proper care of his animals, they have fallen sick; and after having inflicted a wound upon one of them he caused the death of one of my donkeys worth --- silver drachmas -- and introducing --- he chased after (?) a donkey which had broken loose --- my remaining animals ---, he was sent away (?) still as of the month Thoth of the 35th year of Caesar with three sacs belonging to me, without handing over to me either the pack-saddles or the other tools of the animals, and because of such a development my donkeys are standing idle. Therefore I submit this document to you in order that after arresting him you send the culprit up to those persons where it is fitting to send up to, and in order that he be forced in their presence to repay to me the price of the donkey and the (expenses of) the idle days of the donkeys and the sacs and the pack-saddles and the tools in view of my reveiving my fitting dues. Farewell.

title: Nomination of liturgists performing dike work
translation_text: (ll. 9-16)... as ekboleus of the public dykes: Panouris, son of Dioskoros, his surety being Ammonios, son of Psenamounis; Ammonios, son of Proous. All (the above mentioned people) from the village of Takona. In the consulate of Rufius Volusianus and Petronius Annianus, viri clarissimi, Pharmouthi 8. We, Aurelius Valerianus and Aurelius -pianus, have submitted it. I, Aurelius Valerianus, have written for my colleague, since he does not know letters.

title: Tax receipt
translation_text: Year 1 of Nero Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus Imperator, in the month Germanicius, (day) -. Agches son of N.N. (has paid) for --- o the same year of money in silver drachmas two, three obols, in total 2 dr., 3 ob.

title: Loan of money
translation_text: Under the consulship of our lords Constantius and Maximianius. Aurelius Ammo--, gymnasiarch of the glorious and most glorious city of the Oxyrhynchites, to Aurelius N.N., chief-priest, councillor of the city of the Oxyrhynchites, greetings. I agree that I owe you and am in debt for three thousand drachmas of imperial silver currency -- which I will repay to you in the month of Epeiph --- one talent -- one talent --- one talent ---.

title: Receipts for deliveries of grain
summary: The remnants of two lines of a second column show that tl1is badly torn and abraded papyrus originally formed part of a roll of receipts. Of the three receipts preserved here, the first two are in an upright, elegant, flowing cursive, the third in a smaller, narrower hand written with a very thin pen. In line 12 the signature of Diogenis is preceded by the Christian crux ansata: cf. xμy in 8. (from ed. pr.)

title: List of fishes
translation_text: Thoth 11; large Thrissa-fishes, 5; Maeotian fish, 2; Choeros-fish, 1; small Thrissa-fishes, 11.

title: Contract for a parachoresis
translation_text: -- Pharmouthi -, in the city of Oxyrhynchus in the Thebaid. Serapion son of Dionysios, Macedonian, acknowledges to Dionysios son of Dionysios, Thracian, belonging to the manly cavalrists --, in the street, that he has ceded to him in accordance with the administrative arrangements of those controlling cavalry affairs and on the basis of memorandum submitted by Sarapion to Perdikkas, the official in charge of the registries of military settlers' land grants, ten arouras of catoecic land belonging to him near ---ro situated in the Upper toparchy, in the kleros of N.N., of which the neighbors are: on the South a dike, on the North the-- of the kleros, on the East the embankment of the kleros, on the West ---, in order that they belong to Dionysios and his offspring, along with the other rights and benefits, irrefutably for all time, --

title: End of a lease
genre: Papyri; Documentary; Lease; Antoninus Pius; Contract

title: End of a lease
translation_text: "Year 2 of Imperator Caesar Gaius Messius Quintus Traianus Decius pius felix and of Quintus Herennius Etruscus Messius Decius and of Gaius Valens Hostillianus Messius Quintus the most August Caesars Augusti, Phaophi 14. (M. 1) I, Aurelius Stephanus, have leased out as described above. (M. 2) I, Aurelius Apollinarios son of Saras, wrote for him as he was unable to write. (M. 3) I, Aurelius Apollona..."

title: Order to Arrest
translation_text: To the archephodos of ...; send up Ammoni-, son of N.N., and Apollonios, son of Sarapion, and N.N. and the mother Tnepher--, in accordance with the complaint of N.N., son/daughter of Apollinarios. XXXXX

title: Order for Transportation
translation_text: Diogenes to N.N. Put on board (of your ship) and hand over – – – from the crop of the sixth indiction for – – – two hundred and for the 1% tax – – and bring the receipt of the transfer – –. Year 83 = 52, Epeiph 10 – –.

title: Account
translation_text: Account of Phoibammon son of Hermias, scribe; on account of the 'name' of Eulogios k. 1 1/8 and for keph(al )800 (myriads) of (denarii); on account of the 'name' Martyrios k. 1 and on account of the 'name' of Phoibammon son of Pettirios k. 1, in total -- <k.> 3 1/8, for expenses (?) 325 -- , in total 2580 myr.den. and for keph(al ) 800 myr.den.; and Phoibammon son of Onnophris (has paid?) 385 myr.den.; and for the price of 2 dipla (has paid) Arion son of Jacob 300 myr.den. and for transportation charges 300 myr.den., together in total 4265 myriads. Of these through Maria, daughter of Asklas, 1875 myr.den. Remainder in total 2400 myr.den. and 5.5 artabs of wheat....

title: Transportation receipt
translation_text: Aurelius Paulos, son of Olympios, to Aurelius N.N., son of Daniel ---- politeuomenos. I received (from you) and put on board (of a ship) from the crop of the 2nd indiction eight artabas of clean wheat, 8 art. in total only. Through me, I, Paulos son of Olympios, have signed. Epeiph 1 of the 2nd indiction.

title: Receipt for a money loan
translation_text: N.N., son of N.N., -- and however I am styled to Theodoros, son of N.N., of the --an tribe and the Epiphaneian deme, greeting. I acknowledge that I have recived from you, in hand out of the house, as an interest-bearing loan with interest at four obols per mna per month a principal sum in the amount of hundred and sixteen (?) silver drachmas which I shall repay to you on the thirtieth of the current month Pachon without any delay, with right of execution existing upon me and all my possessions just as if resulting from trial. In the third year of Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus and Imperator Caesar Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus, Pachon the twentieth. This note of hand, written in two copies by me, must be authoritative anywehre produced for anyone producing it and it is free from erasure or insertion --

title: Account of expenses / Medical Prescriptions
translation_text: (ll. 10-27) ...; (for) 3.25 ounces of red lead, 3 dr., 3 obols; for .5 (dr. ?) of resin, 1 dr.; for .5 kotyle of oil, 4 ob.; for wood, 2 ob. Headache: for 20 dr. (weight) of red lead, 1 dr., 3 ob.; for 4 dr. (weight) of rust, 1 dr.; for .25 (kotyle) of oil 2 ob.; for vinegar, 1 ob. Fit of fever: for 8 + dr. (weight) of red lead, 1 dr.; for 1 kotyle of oil, 1 dr. 2 ob.; for 28 dr. (weight) of wax, 2 dr., total 4 dr., 1 ob.. grand total 21 dr., 2 ob.; for 3 quantities (or 1 ounce?) of white powder of arsenic sulphides of cobalt, 12 dr.

title: Account of tax collections in barley
summary:
This document is written on the back of the blank part of 3, probably in the same hand, although
                                  the script is larger and written with a blunter pen. The text, which occupies only the left half of the sheet, is a brief account of tax payments in barley in the Ptolemais sector of Karanis' territory (on which cf. PCair Isid 47, 17n.). If, as seems probable from its position, the figure in line 12 is the total of the column, the individual amounts (which abrasion has rendered mostly illegible) were small. On the date of 16, see the intr. to 12.•

title: Fragments of a letter, pen trials and a protocol
summary: Recto contains the fragmentary middle part of a letter starting with the basmalla, followed by well-known opening blessings on the addressee ('May God increase your power'). The sender makes a request of the addressee ('God willing'). Preceding the basmalla some pen trials in the form of well-known blessings appear ('may God prolong your life and may He increase your power'). The verso contains two lines of blessings ('may God strengthen you and may He prolong your life') and also has traces of a protocol.

title: Fragment of a Document
summary: The recto contains the right half of a Coptic document.

title: Fragment of a Document
summary: The recto contains a fragment of a Coptic document.

title: Legal Document
summary: The recto contains the beginning of a legal document preceded by a sign of the cross and ending with witness statements which are also preceded by crosses. There are several abbreviations used in the text.

title: Religous Text
summary: The recto contains the top right corner of a text with religious contents. Mentioning 'the power' and 'the lord.'

title: Letter or Legal Document
summary: The recto contains the end of a letter with a greeting preserved at the end ('I greet you] through these lines of writing') or a legal document in which case the text ends with a witnessstatement ('through these lines').

title: Letter
summary: The recto contains the right half of a Coptic letter.

title: Receipt
summary: The recto contains the end of a receipt with the last line containing Greek numerals.

title: Letter
summary: The recto contains a badly worm-eaten lower part of a Coptic letter with only the final greetings preserved.

title: Writing Exercise
summary: The recto contains the beginning of lines of writing exercises including the expression 'in the name.'

title: Receipts
summary: The recto and verso contain fragments of receipts using Greek numerals.

title: Fragment
summary: The recto contains a fragment of a Coptic text.

title: Fragment
summary: The verso contains a fragment of a Coptic text.

title: Fragment
summary: The recto contains a fragment of a Coptic text.

title: Fragment of a Document
summary: The recto contains the end of a Coptic document.

title: Literary Text
summary: Recto and verso contain parts of the same literary text. On recto 'the earth' is mentioned.

title: Letter
summary: Fragment of a letter dealing with agricultural matters ('there remains to be paid by them, except the clover') and listing amounts of dirhams.

title: Receipt
summary: Top left corner of a receipt for agricultural taxes for 1 1/2 dinar, using Greek numerals in the summary preceding the text.

title: Letter
summary: Bottom half of a letter, without the basmalla but starting with opening greetings ('[may God] complete your [strenghth] and may He make me your ransom for all evil').

title: List
summary: List in which traces of Arabic words can be seen, noting the total of some amounts ('and that makes'), but with only the Greek numerals clearly preserved.

title: List
summary: List of Coptic and Muslim names (Ahmad, Mina).

title: Receipt
summary: Fragment of a Coptic receipt with Greek numerals.

title: Address
summary: Right half of an address containing the name of the addressee ('To Abu al-Hasan, may God preserve him').

title: Fragment
summary: One line of a fragment of a texT.

title: Religious Text
summary: One line of a fragment of a religious text in which the addressee is encouraged to 'act according to Islam.'

title: Letter
summary: Recto contains a fragment of an early administrative letter mentionig that someone has mentioned something ('he mentioned it').

title: Letter
summary: Recto contains the beginning of a letter with part of the basmalla and an opening greeting ('may God prolong your life'). The verso contains only two Arabic letters.

title: Fragments of Two Lists
summary: Recto and verso contain lists using Greek numerals. On the recto the word 'account' can be read and payments made by indiviudals (adda). The verso contains only Greek numerals.

title: Letter
summary: Recto contains the beginning of a letter starting with the basmalla, opening greetings ('may God prolong your life') and a remark about the well-being of the sender's family ('I am writing to you, may [God] prolong [your life, while those with me, are well, God be praised and] thanked'). The letter mentions that the addressee has been appointed over something.

title: Letter
summary: The verso contains the middle part of a letter mentioning that the sender has informed the addressees of something.

title: Letter
summary: The recto contains the middle part of a letter mentioning that someone or something has gone to Fustat.

title: List
summary: The recto contains the middle part of a list mentioning twice the same amount of 1/4 and 1/6 of a dinar and a personal name (Jumdan).

title: List
summary: Of the list on the verso only a nisba (al-Natruni) and part of the amount 1/6 have been preserved.

title: List
summary: The recto contains a fragment of a letter in which the sender orders the addressee to take something from a certain Muhammad and mentions that something has happened repeatedly ('more than one time'). The letter also mentions a servant.

title: Fragment
summary: The recto contains a fragment of an Arabic text mentioning that something amounted to five.

title: Fragment of a Document
summary: The verso contains a fragment of an Arabic text mentioning that someone expelled someone ('he expelled him').

title: Fragment of a Document
summary: The recto contains a fragment of an Arabic text mentioning the reason that something happend ('because of this reason').

title: Fragment
summary: The recto contains a fragment of an Arabic text mentioning the reason that something happend ('because of this reason').

title: Letter
summary: The recto contains the end of a letter mentioning a woman and recording a polite request made to the addressee ('if you see fit to do so, then do this] successfully, God willing').

title: List
summary: The verso contains a list of agricultural taxes (kharaj) with Greek numerals. The list is preceded by a horizontal line at the top of the papyrus.

title: List
summary: The recto contains a list mentioning 'of this remain for us.'

title: Fragments
summary: Recto and verso contain fragments of Arabic texts.

title: Letter
summary: Recto contains the end of a letter to the sender's father ('may God prolong the life of the father'). Part of the address of the letter on recto is written on verso. The addressee's name seems to be Abu al-Qasim.

title: Letter
summary: Verso contains 6 lines belonging to a letter and 1 line in the upper margin which contains the address of the letter on recto. The adressee's name of the letter on verso is Abu Ibrahim. The sender makes several requests ('God willing').

title: Fragment
summary: Recto contains a very effaced complete text preceded by a cross at the top of the papyrus.

title: Fragments
summary: Recto and verso contain fragments of Arabic texts.

title: Fragments of Two Lists
summary: Recto and verso contain fragments of lists. On recto a Coptic name can be read (Shenuda). On verso the Arabic text is completely effaced and only Greek numerals are visible.

title: Letter
summary: Recto contains a fragment of a letter mentioning Satan and the wish that God wards off some evil from a woman. The letter also mentions that something take place in the current month ('in this month').

title: Receipt
summary: Verso contains the end of a receipt with Greek numerals.

title: Fragments
summary: Recto and verso contain fragments of Coptic texts.

title: Letter
summary: Recto contains the end of a Coptic letter in which the sender thanks the addressee for something.

title: Fragments
summary: Recto and verso contain fragments of Coptic texts.

title: Fragment
summary: Recto contains a fragment of a Coptic text.

title: Fragment
summary: Recto contains a fragment of a Coptic text.

title: Fragment
summary: Recto contains the left bottom corner of a Coptic text.

title: Letter
summary: Recto contains the right half of a Coptic letter.

title: Fragment
summary: Recto contains a fragment of a Coptic text.

title: Letter
summary: Recto contains the left half of a Coptic letter.

title: Fragment
summary: Recto contains a fragment of a Coptic text.

title: Fragments
summary: Recto contains a fragment of a Coptic text. Verso has traces of letters in 1 line only.

title: Letter
summary: Recto and verso contain part of a Coptic letter mentioning 'Christ' written in an abbreviated manner. The text on verso is written at a 180 degree angle compared to recto.

title: Fragment
summary: Recto contains a fragment of a Coptic text.

title: Letter
summary: Recto contains a fragment of a letter mentioning that someone has left.

title: Letter
summary: Verso contains the end of a letter with the final greetings to the addressee preserved.

title: Letter
summary: Recto contains a fragment of an Arabic letter in which the sender writes that he has informed the addressee of something.

title: Fragment
summary: Recto contains a fragment of an Arabic text.

title: Letter
summary: Recto contains the right half of a letter, mentioning the sender's desire ('I would like it').

title: List
summary: Verso contains the left half of a list of which only the Greek numerals are preserved.

title: Letter
summary: Recto contains the bottom half of a commercial letter. The sender mentions a shipper called Sam'un. The sender also writes that he is in need of 'true velvet' ('you know, may God preserve you, my need for true velvet'). The letter ends with greetings to the adressee and several other individuals (Abu Ishaq, Abu Muhammad).

title: List
summary: Recto contains a list of personal names and Greek numerals. Verso contains only traces of letters.

title: Letter
summary: Recto the right bottom corner of a letter addressed to 'my lord.' Only the final blessings on the addressee are preserved ('] and may he increase His [goodness on you').

title: Letter
summary: Verso contains a short complete, but badly effaced note written at a 90 degree angle compared to the text on the recto.

title: Writing Exercise
summary: Recto and verso contain the basmalla only, possibly part of a writing exercise. On the recto the basmalla is divided over two lines.

title: Letter
summary: Recto contains the top left corner of a letter with opening blessings for the addressee and mentioning 'day labourers.' On the verso part of the address is preserved with the names of the addressees (Abu Wahib and Abu al-Hasan).

title: Letter
summary: Recto contains the middle part of a letter with some opening greetings on the addressee preserved ('may He fulfill His blessings on you') and mentioning a servant. The sender also mentions that someone owes something to someone else.

title: List
summary: Recto contains the middle part of a Coptic list with Greek numerals.

title: Letter
summary: Verso contains the beginning of a letter in which only the opening blessings on the addressee are preserved ('I have been made your ransom [for all evil]'). The sender excuses himself for writing on a recycled bit of papyrus in a line above the basmalla ('Excuse me, may God strengthen you, for the papyrus').

title: Letter
summary: Recto contains the middle part of a commercial letter referring to amounts of money in dirhams. The sender writes what the addressee should do once he has received something ('and when you receive it, I would like it that ['). An amount of 322 dirhams is said to have been in the town of Ashmun. Some other products are to be delivered to the addressee's as well.

title: Marriage Contract
summary: Recto contains the end of a marriage contract. The name of the groom is Samwil b. Zakariya. Several witness statements are preserved as well.

title: Letter
summary: Recto contains the bottom right corner of a letter mentioning the departure of something or someone.

title: Letter
summary: Recto contains the top part of a commercial letter in which the sender announces that he has received something and that something has happened which was already known to the addressee ('which you already knew, may [God] strengthen you').

title: Letter
summary: Verso contains the bottom left corner of a letter mentioning a journey of 4?.

title: Letter
summary: Recto contains the top half of a note ordering the addressee to pay the sender half a dirham ('pay me half a dirham').

title: Fragment
summary: Verso contains only the Greek numerals that are often found at the bottom of agricultural receipts.

title: Fragment
summary: Recto preserves the starts of 9 lines along the fibers, the right half of a letter in which the sender asks the addressee about his opinion concerning a 'good day' ('and -give me- your opinion, may God strenghten you, concerning the good day'). The verso has the remains of one line, against the fibers.

title: Letter
summary: Recto contains the right bottom corner of a letter mentioning that a certain Kals, the shipper, lived somewhere in the year 24X. This Kals is supposed to return a girl. The letter also mentions a fugitive from amongst certain inhabitants.

title: List
summary: Verso contains a Coptic account with Greek numerals.

title: Letter
summary: Recto contains the beginning part of a badly worm-eaten letter. The opening greetings for the addressee are preserved ('may God prolong your [life]') after the basmalla. The addressee's house is mentioned.

title: List
summary: Recto contains two columns of payments in fractions of amounts noted in Greek numerals made by Coptic individuals some accompanied by their professions (e.g. 'Anyas the guard'). Some make the payments on their own behalf.

title: Letter
summary: Recto contains the right half of a letter with only a few words on two lines preserved.

title: Letter
summary: Verso contains a the left half of a letter with part of the opening blessings on the addressee preserved ('may He make me your ransom for evil'). The sender writes that he does not know about something.

title: Letter
summary: The recto contains the middle part of a badly effaced letter mentioning individuals with their professions.

title: Fragment of a Document
summary: The recto contains the bottom left corner of a document mentioning an amount ('ten dinars and a half and a quarter') and possibly ending with a date ('of the year 6 . .').

title: Letter
summary: The recto contains the middle part of a letter in which the sender expresses his wish about something in two different expressions ('[if you ....] Saturday, then do so' and 'God willing').

title: Letter
summary: The verso contains the right half of a list of writing exercises consisting of blessings used in letters ('may He increase His goodness on you') and witness statements using every time the same personal name (''Abd Allah was witness').

title: Letter
summary: The recto contains the bottom half of a letter with the final blessings on a third person ('may He increase his goodness on him and ameliorate for him').

title: Fragment
summary: The recto contains the middle part of a text using Greek numerals.

title: Letter
summary: The verso contains left upper corner of a letter with the basmalla and part of some opening greetings preserved.

title: Letter
summary: The recto contains the fragmentary right half of a letter in which the sender asks the addressee for his opinion about something ('and your opinion concerning [').

title: Letter
summary: The recto contains the beginning of a letter preserving opening greetings ('[may He amelior]ate His blessing on you') and a remark about the sender's family's well-being ('[and those who are with me] are in good health, praise be [to God]'). The sender hopes for something. The verso preserves part of the address with the name of the addressee as Abu al-Fadl.

title: Letter
summary: The recto contains the left bottom corner of a letter mentioning that the sender has not taken something from some unknown products. The sender also writes that he informs the addressee of something and mentions a third person's partners.

title: Letter
summary: The verso contains the middle part of a letter from a son to his mother. The son asks the mother to favour him, seemingly concerning some financial matters.

title: Letter
summary: The recto contains the right half of a letter referring to the fabrication of something. The sender mentions also that he has obtained something.

title: Letter
summary: The verso contains the middle part of a letter mentioning that the sender will receive the weight of ten ratls of some produce.

title: Letter
summary: The recto contains the left part of a badly frayed letter.

title: Letter
summary: The verso contains the middle part of a badly damaged letter. The sender asks the addressee to give his consent and mentions some business or relationship.

title: Legal Document
summary: The recto contains the beginning of a debt acknowledgement with other texts added and superimposed. There are three lines of a Greek text including numerals written after the Arabic. Several geometric figures were drawn finally at the top of the papyrus.

title: Fragment of a Letter
summary: The recto contains the end of a fragment of an Arabic text mentioning something in the possession of the sender.

title: Letter
summary: The recto contains the middle part of a letter in which the sender mentions that he has not received any answer concerning some things from a third person. The price of sugar cane at the addressee's is mentioned as well.

title: List
summary: The verso contains a list of names and amounts of money using Greek numerals ('Jirje one dirham'). In the upper left corner are some Coptic letters preserved.

title: Lists
summary: Recto and verso contain lists of contents measures (irdabb) and Greek numerals. Some entries have been crossed out.

title: Letter
summary: Recto contains a fragment of a letter in which the sender swears that he will finish his work ('by God, I will do the work' and 'untill I have completed all the work').

title: Letter
summary: Recto contains the beginning of a letter addressed to 'my lord and master.' The sender 'asks from God that he is favourable to the addressee.' A 'favourable answer' is mentioned, presumably from the addressee in response to a request from the sender.

title: List
summary: Verso contains the top of a list of personal names and Greek numerals.

title: Lists
summary: Recto and verso contain lists using Greek numerals. On the verso only the Greek numerals are preserved The recto contains an account of amounts paid and received by a certain Abu 'Ali b. Jalil. Entries record what he received for 'wheat that he brought,' for 'what he received from the house of [. . .],' and what the total amounts to ('to his credit amounts after the invoice').

title: Lists
summary: Recto and verso contain lists of personal names, sometimes including a profession ('priest'), agricultural produce cultivated and Greek numerals. The list is probably related to agricultural taxes.

title: Fragment of a Document
summary: Recto contains a fragment of a text repeating twice the phrase 'concerning that which remains to me at the credit of his heirs' followed by Greek numerals and the phrase 'the case is under consideration in the name of Jirje b. . . .' A scribe is mentioned in relation to a certain Ahmad.

title: Receipt
summary: Verso contains the beginning of a receipt with only the basmalla and the amount of taxes paid preserved using Greek numerals.

title: Letter
summary: Recto and verso contain fragments of very badly damaged letters.

title: Letter
summary: Recto and verso contain fragments of the same letter. The text on verso is written at a 180 degree angle compared to that on the recto. The same person al-Hajjaj b. Muhammad is mentioned on both sides of the papyrus. On the recto a reference to two witnesses is made and something owed by a third person.

title: Letter
summary: Recto contains the right half of a letter mentioning the addressee's letter for a third person ('with your letter to Abu al-Qasim, may God prolong [his life]'). The sender also swears that he will fine someone ('and by God, I will fine [ . . .').

title: Letter
summary: Verso contains the middle part of a letter mentioning that the sender is in dear need and turns to the addressee for help. The sender also writes that he will inform a third person of the addressee's attitude towards the sender.

title: Fragment
summary: The writing on the recto resembles archaic Arabic writing executed in a very unpractised hand which does not allow for any complete words to be recognised. Perhaps it is a modern fake imitation of such writing.
